message.content:
I need to understand the following in the shatter codebase:

1. ExploreConfig struct in shatter-core/src/explorer.rs - full definition and how it's used
2. The explore_function and explore_function_concolic functions - their signatures, loop structure, and how raw_results is built
3. MockParam and MockConfig types in protocol.rs
4. generate_mock_values function in input_gen.rs - signature and what it does
5. How mock_configs/mocks are currently used in explorer.rs
6. The raw_results type and how it feeds into behavior maps

Be thorough - read the full explorer.rs, relevant parts of protocol.rs and input_gen.rs.

78→pub struct ExploreConfig {
    79→    /// Path to the source file being explored (needed for instrumentation).
    80→    pub file: String,
    81→    /// Maximum number of iterations (execute calls) per function.
    82→    pub max_iterations: u32,
    83→    /// Random seed for reproducibility. If None, uses entropy.
    84→    pub seed: Option<u64>,
    85→    /// Mock configurations to pass to Execute commands.
    86→    pub mocks: Vec<MockConfig>,
    87→    /// Path to the setup file, if configured.
    88→    pub setup_file: Option<String>,
    89→    /// When to run setup relative to executions.
    90→    pub setup_level: SetupLevel,
    91→    /// Where each parameter's value should come from.
    92→    pub value_sources: Vec<ValueSource>,
    93→    /// Frontend capabilities (used to gate setup/generate commands).
    94→    pub capabilities: FrontendCapabilities,
    95→    /// User-provided candidate inputs from --inputs or .shatter/ config.
    96→    /// Executed first (highest priority), with no budget cap.
    97→    pub user_seeds: Vec<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
    98→    /// Resolved candidate inputs (from --inputs or .shatter/ config).
    99→    /// Priority above pool seeds, below literal seeds.
   100→    pub candidate_inputs: Vec<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   101→    /// Pre-computed pool seed candidates (from interesting input pool).
   102→    /// Injected after literal candidates but before random generation.
   103→    pub pool_seeds: Vec<Vec<serde_json::Value>>,
   104→    /// Detected project root directory, passed to frontend commands.
   105→    pub project_root: Option<String>,
   106→    /// Iteration count bucket boundaries for loop-aware path hashing.
   107→    pub loop_buckets: LoopBuckets,
   108→    /// Per-function exploration wall-clock timeout. Whichever of this or
   109→    /// `max_iterations` triggers first stops the loop.
   110→    pub timeout_explore: Option<Duration>,
   111→}
   112→
   113→/// Summary of a single function execution during exploration.
   114→#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   115→pub struct ExecutionSummary {
   116→    /// The input values sent to the function.
   117→    pub inputs: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   118→    /// Return value, if the function returned normally.
   119→    pub return_value: Option<serde_json::Value>,
   120→    /// Error message, if the function threw.
   121→    pub thrown_error: Option<String>,
   122→    /// Lines executed during this call.
   123→    pub lines_executed: Vec<u32>,
   124→    /// Whether this execution discovered a new unique path.
   125→    pub is_new_path: bool,
   126→    /// Inferred error intent (validation vs runtime), if an error was thrown.
   127→    pub error_intent: Option<ErrorIntentLabel>,

164→pub struct MockConfig {
   165→    /// Fully qualified symbol name of the dependency to mock.
   166→    pub symbol: String,
   167→    /// Pre-configured return values to cycle through.
   168→    pub return_values: Vec<serde_json::Value>,
   169→    /// Whether to record arguments passed to the mock.
   170→    pub should_track_calls: bool,
   171→    /// Default behavior when return_values are exhausted.
   172→    pub default_behavior: MockBehavior,
   173→}
   174→
   175→/// How a mock should behave when its pre-configured return values are exhausted.
   176→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   177→#[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
   178→pub enum MockBehavior {
   179→    /// Return a generated value based on the return type.
   180→    ReturnGenerated,
   181→    /// Repeat the last return value.
   182→    RepeatLast,
   183→    /// Throw an error.
   184→    ThrowError,
   185→    /// Call through to the real implementation.
   186→    Passthrough,
   187→}
   188→
   189→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   190→// Response: Frontend → Core
   191→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   192→
   193→/// A response message sent from a language frontend to the core engine.
   194→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   195→pub struct Response {
   196→    /// Protocol version for compatibility checking.
   197→    pub protocol_version: String,
   198→    /// Request ID this response corresponds to.
   199→    pub id: u64,
   200→    /// The response payload.
   201→    #[serde(flatten)]
   202→    pub result: ResponseResult,
   203→}
   204→
   205→/// Response payloads from a frontend.
   206→#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Serialize, Deserialize)]
   207→#[serde(tag = "status", rename_all = "snake_case")]
   208→pub enum ResponseResult {
   209→    /// Successful handshake response.
   210→    Handshake {
   211→        /// Protocol version the frontend supports.
   212→        frontend_version: String,
   213→        /// Language this frontend handles (e.g., "typescript", "go").
   214→        language: String,
   215→        /// Capabilities supported by the frontend.
   216→        capabilities: Vec<String>,
   217→    },
   218→    /// Successful analysis result.
   219→    Analyze {
   220→        /// Functions found and analyzed.
   221→        #[serde(default)]
   222→        functions: Vec<FunctionAnalysis>,
   223→    },
   224→    /// Successful instrumentation result.
   225→    Instrument {
   226→        /// Whether instrumentation succeeded.
   227→        instrumented: bool,
   228→        /// Path to the instrumented output file, if applicable.
   229→        output_file: Option<String>,
   230→        /// Number of executable statement lines the instrumentor inserted record calls for.
   231→        /// Used as the denominator for line coverage instead of raw source span.
   232→        #[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
   233→        instrumentable_line_count: Option<u32>,
   234→    },
   235→    /// Successful execution result (boxed to reduce enum size).
   236→    Execute(Box<ExecuteResult>),
   237→    /// Successful setup result.
   238→    Setup {
   239→        /// Opaque context to pass to subsequent Execute commands.
   240→        setup_context: serde_json::Value,
   241→    },
   242→    /// Acknowledgment that teardown completed.
   243→    TeardownAck,
